title: Vertical destination and treeline lift phase
summary: Forty-sixth structured packet for Eco Explorer. This lane-3 wave makes the forest giant-tree line feel more destination-ready, then starts a second vertical identity in treeline with one compact lee-side ascent proof.

execution_order.goal: Prepare the forest giant-tree destination loop handoff.
execution_order.why_now: The forest vertical family is strong enough for a more destination-like follow-on.
A stronger destination loop will make lane 3 feel more outing-grade.
The space needs to stay readable and recoverable while becoming more special.

execution_order.goal: Prepare the first treeline lee-side ascent proof.
execution_order.why_now: Lane 3 should begin a second vertical family instead of only extending forest.
A small treeline shelter ascent will broaden vertical identity without too much risk.
Treeline should gain a promising vertical beat without losing its calm readability.

guardrails: Keep the spaces cozy and recoverable.
Reuse the current tiny cue language.
Do not open a harsher traversal shell or denser HUD.
Keep the treeline proof compact and shelter-oriented.
acceptance_focus: The forest giant-tree line feels more destination-ready.
Treeline gains the beginning of its own vertical identity.
Lane 3 grows in variety without turning harsh.
handoff_prompt: Read AGENTS.md, .agents/lane-runner.md, .agents/project-memory.md, .agents/work-queue.md, .agents/lanes/lane-3.md, docs/reports/2026-03-31-vertical-destination-and-treeline-lift-phase.md, and this packet. Treat this as lane 3's next wave: make the forest giant-tree line more destination-ready, then add one compact treeline lee-side ascent proof while keeping the spaces cozy and recoverable.
